scanSteps.label: Docker reclaimable layers
scanSteps.detail: Checking unused Docker images, exited containers, and safe prune candidates.

scanSteps.label: Safe cache and log noise
scanSteps.detail: Reviewing regenerable cache and log surfaces DreamCleanr already handles conservatively.

scanSteps.label: Stale helper residue
scanSteps.detail: Finding dormant helper and probe residue that no longer needs to hang around.

scanSteps.label: Protected AI state held back
scanSteps.detail: Flagging Claude, Codex, and protected Docker state for visibility without treating it as safe cleanup.

results.title: You could safely reclaim 18.9GB from known-safe surfaces right now.
results.summary: The sample machine surfaces 21.8GB in total, but DreamCleanr holds back 2.9GB of protected AI state instead of pretending everything is safe to delete.

breakdown.label: Docker reclaimable
breakdown.note: Unused images, exited containers, and safe prune candidates.

breakdown.label: Cache and logs
breakdown.note: Regenerable developer cache noise and old log buildup.

breakdown.label: Helper residue
breakdown.note: Stale helper chains and leftover probes worth cleaning up.

breakdown.label: Protected AI state
breakdown.note: Visible for context, but intentionally excluded from automatic cleanup.

firstWin.title: Lead with a real local scan and a visible receipt.
firstWin.copy: The browser demo stops at the sample result. In the actual free product, the first win is a local CLI or MCP scan, a preview-first cleanup, and a one-page receipt you can inspect.

upgrade.title: Want guided cleanup, richer history, and premium workflows?
upgrade.copy: Keep using free DreamCleanr today, then raise your hand for the one-time premium macOS shell when guided cleanup, presets, and richer local history are ready.
